commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch expands Codex OAuth refresh-failure handling, changes OpenAI/Codex auth profile selection, and updates related diagnostics, tests, and changelog entries.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: a direct `runEmbeddedPiAgent` call with provider `openai`, PI runtime, `authProfileIdSource: auto`, and a stale `openai-codex:*` profile still returns that requested profile before eligibility checks. I did not run a live revoked-token reproduction in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can address the blocking source-level bug by eligibility-checking auto-selected runtime profiles before provider switching.

target.title: fix(auth): drop stale Codex OAuth routing
target.body:
## Summary

- expand OAuth refresh-failure classification to match current Codex refresh-token reuse, expiry, invalidation, revocation, and account-mismatch messages
- remove the legacy Codex OAuth sidecar/Keychain write path while preserving read-only macOS Keychain recovery for legacy `oauthRef` sidecars until doctor can migrate them inline
- keep stale persisted/session-selected Codex auth profiles and stale `auth.order.openai` entries from switching OpenAI PI runs to `openai-codex`
- skip Codex harness auth bootstrap when there is no eligible forwarded plugin harness profile
- add changelog coverage for the Codex auth behavior and fold the #83345 fix into this PR

Fixes #83223.
Supersedes #83345.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.hints.test.ts src/commands/status-all/gateway.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted-boundary.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.e2e.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.test.ts`: passed, 14 files / 231 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted-boundary.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-oauth-sidecar.test.ts`: passed, 3 files / 14 tests
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted-boundary.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-oauth-sidecar.test.ts && git diff --check`: passed
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `check:changed`: passed on `tbx_01krwqf7wrzmhzpq4rxx5qk78t`, Actions run `https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26014548084`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server OAuth refresh failures now classify current Codex terminal refresh-token states precisely while user-facing messages explain that OpenClaw cannot use the user's OpenAI account for Codex until they sign in again. Generic account-state drift remains retryable/non-terminal. Stale Codex profile references from prior config/session state no longer force OpenAI PI runs onto the `openai-codex` provider when the requested route is `openai/gpt-5.5`. Legacy sidecar-backed Codex OAuth profiles remain recoverable through the read-only macOS Keychain seed fallback and can still be migrated by `openclaw doctor --fix`.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox via Crabbox wrapper using the OpenClaw CI check workflow.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -provider blacksmith-testbox -blacksmith-workflow ci-check-testbox.yml -blacksmith-job check -keep-on-failure --shell -- "git fetch origin main && pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: focused regression suites passed locally in the Codex worktree, and `check:changed` completed successfully on Testbox `tbx_01krwqf7wrzmhzpq4rxx5qk78t` for commit `90df0ef6b50`.

Observed result after fix: OAuth copy/classifier, stale profile boundary, legacy sidecar Keychain recovery, Codex app-server auth bridge, doctor/status messaging, stale OpenAI PI routing, and harness bootstrap regression coverage all pass; the remote changed gate passes typecheck, lint, import-cycle, and changed-surface checks.

What was not tested: a live Docker session with a real revoked/expired OpenAI refresh token was not reproduced; coverage is source-level parity with current Codex messages plus mocked OAuth refresh paths and focused stale-routing/legacy-sidecar regression tests.
